answer:
Verdict: Criminal risk low. Civil risk high. Business-death risk real if LinkedIn data core to revenue. Two companies before you — hiQ, Proxycurl — both dead from this exact fight.

**Where you stand:**

1. **CFAA (criminal hacking law): weak threat — IF you never log in.** Ninth Circuit in [hiQ v. LinkedIn](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HiQ_Labs_v._LinkedIn) said scraping truly public pages likely not "without authorization." But hiQ still lost war: [final ruling](https://www.privacyworld.blog/2022/11/federal-court-rules-in-favor-of-linkedins-breach-of-contract-claim-after-six-years-of-cfaa-data-scraping-litigation/) went for LinkedIn on contract, and hiQ admitted CFAA liability for fake accounts hitting logged-in pages. Settlement: permanent injunction, delete all data + code + models, $500K. Company gone.

2. **Breach of contract: your biggest legal exposure.** LinkedIn ToS bans scraping. If you or your crawlers ever accepted ToS — created account, logged in, clicked through — contract claim sticks. Logged-out-only scraping much safer here (Bright Data beat Meta on this theory in 2024 — no account, no contract).

3. **Fake/burner accounts = existential.** [LinkedIn sued Proxycurl January 2025](https://www.socialmediatoday.com/news/linkedin-wins-legal-case-data-scrapers-proxycurl/756101/) (N.D. Cal. 3:25-cv-00828) over "hundreds of thousands" fake accounts. [Settled mid-2025: permanent injunction, delete everything, company shut down](https://www.startuphub.ai/ai-news/startup-news/2025/the-1-linkedin-scraping-startup-proxycurl-shuts-down). Proxycurl had $10M ARR. Dead anyway. Founder [wrote postmortem](https://nubela.co/blog/is-scraping-linkedin-legal-in-2026/) — read it.

4. **Other exposure:** State privacy laws (CCPA etc.) mostly exempt "publicly available" data, but you resell personal data → likely a "data broker," registration duties in CA/TX/OR/VT, deletion requests via California DELETE Act. Right-of-publicity claims possible in some states. Also fraud/tortious-interference claims if you evade IP blocks or CAPTCHAs — circumvention makes everything worse.

**What changes your fate:**
- Never authenticate. No accounts, no fake profiles, no session cookies. This is line between "risky" and "doomed."
- Don't circumvent technical blocks after cease-and-desist. Continuing post-C&D revives CFAA theory.
- Assume C&D letter coming if you grow. LinkedIn/Microsoft litigates aggressively and wins by attrition — hiQ spent 6 years, died anyway.
- Structural hedge: diversify data sources (licensed vendors, first-party enrichment, public web beyond LinkedIn) so injunction not kill company. Proxycurl: half revenue LinkedIn-dependent, that was fatal.
- Get real counsel before scaling. This is not settled-law-safe territory — it is "big adversary chooses when to end you" territory.

Sources: [hiQ v. LinkedIn (Wikipedia)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HiQ_Labs_v._LinkedIn), [Privacy World on hiQ contract ruling](https://www.privacyworld.blog/2022/11/federal-court-rules-in-favor-of-linkedins-breach-of-contract-claim-after-six-years-of-cfaa-data-scraping-litigation/), [Social Media Today on Proxycurl case](https://www.socialmediatoday.com/news/linkedin-wins-legal-case-data-scrapers-proxycurl/756101/), [StartupHub on Proxycurl shutdown](https://www.startuphub.ai/ai-news/startup-news/2025/the-1-linkedin-scraping-startup-proxycurl-shuts-down), [Proxycurl founder's account](https://nubela.co/blog/is-scraping-linkedin-legal-in-2026/), [Jenner & Block CFAA alert](https://www.jenner.com/en/news-insights/publications/client-alert-data-scraping-in-hiq-v-linkedin-the-ninth-circuit-reaffirms-narrow-interpretation-of-cfaa)
